reasoning: Multiple books have Detroit as a heavy favorite (-199 to -210) with betting models giving the Tigers roughly a 62-63% win probability, and SportsGrid/BetMGM previews cite Troy Melton's 5-1 record, 1.80 ERA and dominance at home (sub-1.00 ERA in his last four Comerica starts per Tony's Picks) as the driving factor. article_consensus: Coverage frames Dobnak as a contact/ground-ball pitcher who is not a strikeout artist, works on a short leash, and has shaky underlying metrics, while Melton is the polar opposite — a hard-throwing, bat-missing rookie who's been dominant at Comerica Park; the shared thread across previews (short Dobnak outings, low total, pitcher-friendly park) points toward fewer strikeouts from Dobnak specifically even as the game overall trends toward a lower-scoring affair.

reasoning: Covers.com lists Atlanta as a 'heavy -223 moneyline favorite behind their ace, Chris Sale,' noting his 2.77 FIP and 0.86 HR/9 over his last four outings, while Canning is described as 'struggling.' article_consensus: Multiple previews (CBS/FLM, Battery Power, Covers, SportsBettingDime) uniformly frame this as a mismatch favoring Atlanta's Chris Sale over San Diego's struggling Griffin Canning (1-8, 6.67 ERA), but the strikeout-specific reporting leans toward a modest total for Sale rather than a huge one -- his best recent start still only produced 6 K's in 7 innings on just 89 pitches, and Braves-focused coverage notes his 'rollercoaster rotation' rarely lets him pitch deep into games.
